figure.title: Virtual Pod Architecture with Trusted Execution Environment
figure.purpose: Illustrates how Virtual Pods incorporate confidential computing components like Trusted VMs and TSMs to ensure security and isolation.

technical_summary: A 'Virtual Pod' spans multiple physical System Nodes (e.g., Node 1 and Node 2).
Each participating System Node runs a 'Trusted VM' containing the Confidential Computing Manager (CCM), Application, Accelerator Driver, and Kernel.
A Trusted Security Module (TSM) interfaces with the Trusted VM.
Each Accelerator within the vPod has a Device Security Manager (DSM).
The green shaded areas represent the Trusted Computing Base (TCB) of the Virtual Pod, which encompasses the Trusted VMs, TSMs, and the Accelerators' internal security contexts.

components.name: Trusted VM
components.description: Secure virtual machine hosting the application and drivers.

critical_facts: Virtual Pods provide hardware-enforced isolation (Confidential Computing), extending the Trusted Computing Base across the network fabric to encompass remote accelerators and host VMs.
